name: baseline_calibrated
description: Default configuration with all pipeline features enabled and global calibration. This is the standard production config against which ablations are compared.

expected_performance.notes: Solid all-around performance with moderate cost. Best balance of accuracy and efficiency.

name: cheap_fast
description: Minimal-cost configuration using only Tier A model with reduced evidence processing. Suitable for high-volume, low-stakes forecasts or when budget is constrained.

expected_performance.notes: Acceptable for screening or triage. Expect 20-40% accuracy degradation vs baseline on hard questions.

name: full_pipeline
description: Maximum-quality configuration using both Tier A and Tier B models, all evidence features, disagreement detection, and domain-specific calibration. Highest accuracy but highest cost.

expected_performance.notes: Best accuracy, especially on hard questions with ambiguous evidence. 3-5x cost of baseline. Use for high-stakes forecasts.

name: benchmark_optimized
description: Configuration tuned from ablation experiments to maximise ForecastBench and Metaculus tournament scores. Uses credibility-weighted evidence, recency weighting, domain calibration, and VoI gating to stay within moderate budget.

expected_performance.notes: Best cost-adjusted performance from ablation study. Domain calibration adds ~2% accuracy at minimal extra cost. Recommended for tournament submission.
